meta.title: Add Mutual Exclusion Validation to CellDocument Builder

architecture_notes: All changes confined to the castep-cell-io crate — no changes to castep_cell_fmt, lib.rs exports, or README.
Use bon's Custom Finishing Function pattern (finish_fn) rather than a separate .validate() method. This bakes validation into build() so callers cannot forget to validate.

The bon derive on CellDocument renames the generated infallible method to build_internal() (private). A manual impl<S: IsComplete> CellDocumentBuilder<S> provides a public build() -> CResult<CellDocument> that calls build_internal() then runs 4 validation rules.

Validation rules check mutual exclusion of at-most-one-Of-N optional fields. The counting idiom uses .is_some() on each field, collected into an array, .iter().filter(|&&x| x).count(), and checks count > 1.
kpoints_mp_offset and spectral_kpoints_mp_offset are NOT mutually exclusive with anything — they are tuning parameters that can coexist with other kpoint specifications.

D2 absorption: bs_kpoint_path and bs_kpoints_list are backward-compat aliases for spectral_kpoint_path and spectral_kpoints_list. They are included in the ^4 mutual-exclusion check (at most 1 of 6), and BS_ parsing in from_cell_file is guarded to prevent double-population.
The #[allow(clippy::duplicated_attributes)] on line 237 must be preserved — the derive(Builder) + #[builder(...)] combination intentionally triggers this clippy lint.

ParamDocument uses a separate .validate() -> Result<Self, String> pattern. This plan deliberately diverges for CellDocument — the fallible builder approach is chosen for one-shot builder usage where the caller cannot forget validation.
known_pitfalls: PITFALL-1 (IsComplete trait visibility): cell_document_builder::IsComplete is a private module generated by the derive macro on CellDocument. The manual impl<S: cell_document_builder::IsComplete> block MUST be placed in the same parent module (cell_document) as the derive, i.e., in the same file (cell_document.rs). If placed elsewhere, the path will not resolve. Compile check 'cargo check -p castep_cell_io' must pass before proceeding.

PITFALL-2 (maybe_ setter naming): For Option<T> fields, bon generates setters prefixed with maybe_ (e.g., maybe_kpoints_list). Do NOT use the non-prefixed name (e.g., .kpoints_list()) — the compiler will error. If 'cargo build' succeeds, the correct setters are used.

PITFALL-3 (Missing optional field in from_cell_file builder): When converting the struct literal to builder calls, if a maybe_* setter is omitted, the field silently becomes None (no compile error). Cross-reference every field in the struct literal against the builder chain. The elaboration lists all 48 optional fields in order — check each one off.

PITFALL-4 (bs_kpoint_path_spacing is NOT mutually exclusive): bs_kpoint_path_spacing is a tuning parameter, not a kpoint specification. Do NOT include it in the ^4 validation check. It is already correctly excluded from the plan.

PITFALL-5 (Test fixture types): LatticeCart, PositionsFrac, and their entry types must be constructible in tests. Use direct struct construction for types without builders (e.g., LatticeCart, PhononKpointPath). Use builder() for types with #[derive(bon::Builder)] (e.g., KpointsList, SpectralKpointPath, SymmetryOps). Import entry-level types (Kpoint, PositionFracEntry, etc.) explicitly in the test module — they are not imported by cell_document.rs's use statements.

PITFALL-6 (bon v3.9.1 version pin): The finish_fn attribute and IsComplete trait naming depend on bon v3.9.1 behavior. The version is pinned in Cargo.toml. If bon is upgraded later, run 'cargo check -p castep_cell_io' immediately to catch breaking changes.

PITFALL-7 (BS_ parse ordering): The bs_kpoint_path/is_some() guard depends on spectral_kpoint_path being parsed first (lines 529-543 come before lines 545-553). This ordering already exists in the current code. Do NOT reorder these parsing blocks.

PITFALL-8 (existing test preservation): The single existing test test_parse_mg2sio4_cell is #[ignore] with an empty input string. New tests should be added to the same #[cfg(test)] mod tests block without modifying or removing the existing test.

task_groups.reason: Converts CellDocument builder to fallible and implements all 4 validation rules. This is a single atomic change — the derive modification and custom build() impl must land together. TDD: unit tests written first, then implementation evolves to satisfy them. All changes are in cell_document.rs.
Updates from_cell_file to add BS_ duplication guards and convert struct literal to builder pattern. Depends on the fallible builder from group-builder-fallible because .build() now returns CResult.

Adds an integration test that exercises the full parse→build pipeline with conflicting k-point blocks. Depends on both the fallible builder and from_cell_file updates being in place. Creates a new file in tests/.

tasks.description: Convert CellDocument builder to fallible using bon's finish_fn pattern and implement 4 mutual-exclusion validation rules in custom build(). Write 10 unit tests first (RED), then implement the derive change and custom build() impl (GREEN).

changes.guidance:
Two coordinated changes to cell_document.rs:

1. MODIFY the #[builder(...)] attribute on CellDocument (line 239): Add finish_fn(vis = "", name = build_internal) to the existing #[builder(on(Lattice, into), on(Positions, into))] attribute. The full attribute becomes: #[builder(on(Lattice, into), on(Positions, into), finish_fn(vis = "", name = build_internal))]. Preserve #[allow(clippy::duplicated_attributes)] on the line above.

2. ADD a new impl block immediately after the CellDocument struct definition (after line 441, before impl FromCellFile for CellDocument). The block starts with 'use cell_document_builder::IsComplete;' then defines 'impl<S: cell_document_builder::IsComplete> CellDocumentBuilder<S> { pub fn build(self) -> CResult<CellDocument> { ... } }'. Inside build(): call self.build_internal() to get the CellDocument, then apply 4 validation checks as count-checks using the array pattern [field.is_some(), ...].iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() > 1. On violation, return Err(Error::Message("...".into())). Rules: ^3 (kpoints_list / kpoints_mp_grid / kpoints_mp_spacing — at most 1 of 3), ^4+D2 (spectral_kpoint_path / spectral_kpoints_mp_grid / spectral_kpoints_mp_spacing / spectral_kpoints_list / bs_kpoint_path / bs_kpoints_list — at most 1 of 6), ^5 (phonon_kpoint_path / phonon_kpoint_list — at most 1 of 2), ^6 (symmetry_generate / symmetry_ops — at most 1 of 2). If all checks pass, return Ok(doc). The Error::Message variant takes String — use .into() on the &str literal.

3. TESTS: Add 10 test functions in the existing #[cfg(test)] mod tests block (starting at line 952). Do NOT remove the existing #[ignore] test test_parse_mg2sio4_cell. Add test-only imports for entry-level types at the top of the test module (Species, PositionFracEntry, Kpoint, SpectralKpointPathEntry, BsKpointPathEntry, PhononKpointPathEntry, PhononKpointListEntry, SymmetryOp). Write helper functions minimal_lattice() and minimal_positions() returning valid Lattice and Positions. Then write the 10 test functions per the tdd_interface.test_code specification — the test code IS the contract.

tdd_interface.expected_behavior: The build() method returns Ok(CellDocument) when all mutual-exclusion constraints are satisfied (at most one field per rule group is Some). It returns Err(Error::Message(...)) when multiple fields from the same rule group are Some. Fields not in any rule group (e.g., kpoints_mp_offset, spectral_kpoints_mp_offset) do NOT participate in validation and can be freely combined. The 4 rule groups tested: ^3 (kpoints_list / kpoints_mp_grid / kpoints_mp_spacing), ^4+D2 (6 spectral/BS fields), ^5 (phonon_kpoint_path / phonon_kpoint_list), ^6 (symmetry_generate / symmetry_ops).

wiring_checklist.detail: CellDocumentBuilder is already exported via pub use cell_document::{CellDocument, CellDocumentBuilder, Lattice, Positions} on line 23. Verify this line is unchanged and the builder re-export still compiles.
README line 35-36 shows .build().unwrap() — verify this example remains correct (they call .unwrap() on the Result). No change expected.

type_reference.CellDocumentBuilder: pub struct CellDocumentBuilder<S> { /* generated by bon */ }
type_reference.IsComplete: trait IsComplete { /* marker trait in generated cell_document_builder module */ }
type_reference.build_internal: fn build_internal(self) -> CellDocument (private, generated by bon)
type_reference.build: pub fn build(self) -> CResult<CellDocument> (manual impl, returns Result with Error::Message on validation failure)

tasks.description: Update from_cell_file in CellDocument to (a) guard BS_ block parsing behind spectral.is_none() checks preventing duplicate field population, and (b) replace the struct literal construction with a builder chain.

changes.guidance:
Make three coordinated changes inside the impl FromCellFile for CellDocument block (starting at line 443). All changes are within the from_cell_file method body — the function signature stays the same:

CHANGE A: PRESERVE the existing lattice mutual-exclusion check at lines 486-491 ("Both LATTICE_CART and LATTICE_ABC are specified"). This parse-time check provides a more specific error message than builder validation would. Do NOT modify or remove it.

CHANGE B: GUARD BS_ block parsing (lines 545-553). The bs_kpoint_path and bs_kpoints_list parsing at lines 545-553 currently unconditionally populate their fields even when the spectral_* variant already parsed the same data (because find_block_any for spectral includes BS_ aliases at lines 529-543). Fix: wrap each bs_* parsing in an if/else guard: if spectral_kpoint_path.is_some() { None } else { parse BS_ block }. Same pattern for bs_kpoints_list: if spectral_kpoints_list.is_some() { None } else { parse BS_ block }. The parsing code inside the else branch remains unchanged — just wrap it with .ok().map(...).transpose()? as before. CRITICAL: do NOT reorder these blocks — spectral blocks are parsed at lines 529-543 (before BS_ blocks at 545-553), and the .is_some() guards depend on this ordering.

CHANGE C: REPLACE the Ok(CellDocument { lattice, positions, ...48 optional fields... }) struct literal at lines 714-765 with a CellDocument::builder() chain ending in .build(). Required fields (lattice, positions) use plain setters. All 48 optional fields use maybe_* setters. The order of setter calls should match the field order in the struct literal exactly. The full list of maybe_ setters in order: maybe_kpoints_list, maybe_bs_kpoint_path, maybe_bs_kpoints_list, maybe_optics_kpoints_list, maybe_magres_kpoints_list, maybe_bs_kpoint_path_spacing, maybe_kpoints_mp_grid, maybe_kpoints_mp_spacing, maybe_kpoints_mp_offset, maybe_spectral_kpoint_path, maybe_spectral_kpoints_list, maybe_spectral_kpoint_path_spacing, maybe_spectral_kpoints_mp_grid, maybe_spectral_kpoints_mp_spacing, maybe_spectral_kpoints_mp_offset, maybe_symmetry_ops, maybe_symmetry_tol, maybe_symmetry_generate, maybe_fix_com, maybe_ionic_constraints, maybe_nonlinear_constraints, maybe_fix_all_ions, maybe_fix_all_cell, maybe_cell_constraints, maybe_fix_vol, maybe_external_efield, maybe_external_pressure, maybe_species_mass, maybe_species_pot, maybe_species_lcao_states, maybe_species_q, maybe_hubbard_u, maybe_sedc_custom_params, maybe_phonon_kpoint_list, maybe_phonon_kpoint_path, maybe_phonon_kpoints_mp_grid, maybe_phonon_kpoints_mp_spacing, maybe_phonon_kpoints_mp_offset, maybe_phonon_fine_kpoint_path, maybe_phonon_fine_kpoint_path_spacing, maybe_phonon_fine_kpoints_mp_grid, maybe_phonon_fine_kpoints_mp_spacing, maybe_phonon_fine_kpoints_mp_offset, maybe_phonon_gamma_directions, maybe_phonon_fine_kpoint_list, maybe_phonon_supercell_matrix, maybe_supercell_kpoint_list, maybe_ionic_velocities. The final .build() call returns CResult<CellDocument> which matches the existing return type — use ? operator or return directly. Remove the old Ok(CellDocument { ... }) struct literal entirely.

wiring_checklist.detail: from_cell_file uses builder .build() which returns CResult<CellDocument>. Verify the ? operator propagates errors correctly and the function still returns CResult<Self>.
Verify no export changes needed — CellDocument, CellDocumentBuilder remain exported.

tasks.description: Add integration test that parses a .cell string containing conflicting KPOINT_LIST and KPOINT_MP_GRID blocks and verifies the parse returns Err.

changes.guidance: Create a new integration test file. The test function should: (1) define a &str input containing a minimal valid .cell file with %BLOCK LATTICE_CART, %BLOCK POSITIONS_FRAC, %BLOCK KPOINTS_LIST, and the keyword KPOINT_MP_GRID : 2 2 2 — so both kpoint-list and kpoint-mp-grid are specified, which violates rule ^3. (2) call castep_cell_fmt::parse::<CellDocument>(input) and assert the result is Err. Import castep_cell_fmt::parse and castep_cell_io::CellDocument. The test function name should describe the scenario, e.g., parse_rejects_conflicting_kpoint_specs.

wiring_checklist.detail: Integration test file compiles and is discovered by cargo test. Verify the test appears in 'cargo test --list' output.
